response.justification: The candidate has a broad background including geospatial work (GISCollective, FOSS4G speaker) and distributed systems exposure (Dunnhumby, high-throughput microservices), which shows some domain adjacency. However, the core technical requirement — modern C++ or Rust — is critically unmet: the candidate's C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor's thesis project, both over a decade old. The primary language stack for the past 10+ years has been TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no Rust experience. There is also no experience with routing pipelines, navigation tile generation, or map data optimization, which are central to the role.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS platform development via GISCollective, including presenting at FOSS4G 2019
Exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby — millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency constraints
Broad systems breadth including Linux, DLang (a systems-level language), and some historical C/C++ work at National Instruments
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust proficiency — the two required languages; primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js for the past decade
C++ experience is from 2014–2015 (brief, ~11 years stale) and not at the level of production routing/geospatial pipelines
No experience with routing tiling, navigation guidance systems, or map data pipeline optimization — the core responsibilities of the role
